name: Big Soft Chocolate Chip Cookies
recipeIngredient: 2 cups Unsifted, Bleached All Purpose Flour
1 cup Light or Dark Brown Sugar
recipeInstructions:
1. Place a rack in the middle of the oven and preheat to 325°F.
Use an oven thermometer to verify the correct temperature.
2. Melt the butter in a small saucepan until it's lukewarm.
3. In a large bowl, whisk together the flour, baking soda, and salt.
4. In a medium mixing bowl, combine both sugars. Pour the melted butter into the sugar mixture and mix until fully blended.
5. Add the egg and yolk, then vanilla extract to the batter. Mix until combined.
6. Gradually add the flour mixture to the batter, mixing until just blended.
7. Fold in the chocolate chips until evenly distributed.
8. Line 11 x 17 cookie sheets with parchment paper (avoid using waxed paper).
9. Using a scant 1/4 cup of dough, form balls and shape them into half-moon halves.
Press the halves together to create cookies with jagged top surfaces.
10. Place about 6 cookies per sheet and bake for 15 minutes.
11. Check for doneness after 13 minutes, then every two minutes until the edges are dry and firm while the centers remain soft.
12. Allow cookies to cool completely on their sheets.
The twin secrets to these "mall style" cookies are the melted butter and the extra egg yolk.
Serving Suggestions: Serve warm or allow to cool completely. Store airtight in containers, separating layers with waxed paper. Refrigerated for 2 days, frozen up to 30 days (wrap tightly in plastic wrap). Thaw frozen dough overnight in the refrigerator. Baked cookies freeze well for a few weeks; wrap individually and thaw at room temperature before eating.
